title: Kalshi Seeks Approval to List Perpetual Futures Tied to Gold
summary: Kalshi Inc. is seeking approval to expand precious-metals trading on its platform with a popular type of derivative that never expires for gold, silver and platinum.

title: Kazakh Oil Flows to Black Sea Forced to Stop as Tankers Hit
summary: Kazakhstan is being forced to stop piping crude to its main export terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast as a spate of attacks on tankers jeopardizes the landlocked Central Asian country’s exports.

title: UK Finance Sees Steady Hand in Healey as New Chancellor
summary: Britain’s financial industry welcomed John Healey’s appointment as chancellor of the exchequer, hoping the Treasury’s seventh occupant this decade will deliver stability.

title: Economic Momentum Flips From Blessing to Curse for S&P 500 Bulls
summary: Strong economic momentum is a double-edged sword for US equity traders. Solid growth is an earnings tailwind. But let it run too hot, and it turns from blessing to curse.

title: BlackRock vs BlackRock: Aston Martin Debt Revamp Risks In-House Clash
summary: A potential debt revamp at Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc is setting the stage for an unusual battle between different divisions of asset management giant BlackRock Inc.

title: Stocks Climb as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid rally in chipmakers on bets the artificial-intelligence trade that’s powered the bull market has more room to run.

title: Nasdaq 100 Soars as Chipmaker Rebound Overshadows Iran War
summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index advanced on Tuesday as a rebound in chipmakers at the center of the artificial-intelligence trade overshadowed the re-escalation in the US-Iran war.

title: London Stock Exchange to Introduce Nonstop Trading Next Year
summary: The London Stock Exchange will open a new venue outside of its regular market hours, a move to offer “near-continuous trading” and better compete with the 24/7 trading provided by alternative platforms.

title: London’s 24-Hour Trading Is Unlikely to Revive Market, GAM Says
summary: The London Stock Exchange’s plan to introduce nearly 24-hour trading for equities may fall short of reviving the bourse’s fortunes, the head of GAM Investments’ global equities team said.

title: Indian Refiners Halt Iraq Oil Loadings as Hormuz Risks Escalate
summary: Two Indian state-run refiners have suspended crude oil loadings from Iraq as mounting security risks in the Strait of Hormuz make it increasingly difficult to ensure safety of vessels, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
summary: Brent oil extended its recent rally as traders weighed threats to maritime traffic from the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea to a key Kazakh export terminal on Russia’s coast.

title: Gold and Silver Rise on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Fed Strategy
summary: Gold and silver rose on dip-buying, as traders monitored Middle East tensions for signs that higher energy costs could stoke inflation, putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten policy.

title: Short Sellers Ramp Up Bets Against HelloFresh’s Debut Bond
summary: Bets against HelloFresh SE’s debut bond, issued only three weeks ago, have surged after its trading got off to a rocky start earlier this month.

title: Colombia, Peru Send Early Policy Signs to Extend Election Rally
summary: Investors are finding fresh reasons to buy Colombian and Peruvian assets as the countries’ newly elected presidents deliver early signals that their market-friendly agendas will translate into policy overhaul.

title: InMobi Appoints JPMorgan, Jefferies for Nearly $1 Billion IPO
summary: InMobi Pte has appointed four banks for an initial public offering over the next few months to raise about $1 billion for the Indian mobile advertising company.

title: Biotech IPOs Outperform AI Listings, Leading US Market in 2026
summary: The biotechnology sector is stealing the US IPO market thunder from artificial intelligence-related listings, delivering standout returns as bankers line up a steady stream of summer debuts.

title: Saudi Crude Flow From Red Sea Hit Record Before Houthi Warning
summary: Saudi Arabia exported record volumes of crude oil from terminals on the Red Sea in the days and weeks before the Houthis said they would block the nation’s shipments.

title: LA’s $1.2 Billion Graffiti Towers Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
summary: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more than seven years after the original Chinese developer ran out of money.

title: Chinese Oil Tanker U-Turns in Red Sea After Houthi Attack Threat
summary: A very large crude carrier has made a U-turn in the southern Red Sea after warnings from Houthi rebels in Yemen that they would target ships heading to or from Saudi ports.

title: Shale Trades Go-Go Days of Growth for Output Creep
summary: US drillers are letting production rise again — just not the way they used to.
